picks.game: San Antonio Spurs vs Oklahoma City Thunder
picks.rationale: The San Antonio Spurs are coming off a dominant 103-82 victory in Game 4, where they held the Oklahoma City Thunder to just 33% shooting. The Thunder are significantly hampered by injuries, with Ajay Mitchell ruled out and Jalen Williams questionable, impacting their offensive flow and secondary scoring. Victor Wembanyama's exceptional play, including 33 points in Game 4, and the Spurs' overall rebounding advantage further bolster their chances to keep this game close. Public betting trends also show a strong lean towards the Spurs on both the spread and moneyline, indicating sharp money is on the underdog.

picks.game: San Antonio Spurs vs Oklahoma City Thunder
picks.rationale: Game 4 of this series saw a drastic drop in scoring, with the final total reaching only 185 points (103-82). The Oklahoma City Thunder's offense has been suppressed due to key injuries to Ajay Mitchell (out) and the questionable status of Jalen Williams, which limits their ball-handling and shot creation. San Antonio's defense, spearheaded by Victor Wembanyama's rim protection, made crucial adjustments in Game 4 to limit Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and perimeter shooting, holding OKC to an inefficient 33% from the field. With the Thunder's offensive struggles and the Spurs' elite postseason defensive rating, a lower-scoring affair is anticipated.

picks.game: San Antonio Spurs vs Oklahoma City Thunder
picks.rationale: Victor Wembanyama has been a dominant force for the Spurs, averaging 30.3 points over his last four games and delivering a 33-point performance in Game 4. As the primary offensive weapon, he is expected to maintain a high scoring volume in this pivotal Game 5, especially given the Thunder's defensive focus on him. Wembanyama is active and a key player for the San Antonio Spurs, making this prop a strong play. His ability to score efficiently and consistently makes him a reliable option to exceed this point total.

picks.game: San Antonio Spurs vs Oklahoma City Thunder
picks.rationale: The San Antonio Spurs have demonstrated their capability to win on the road in this series, having secured a Game 1 victory in double overtime as significant underdogs. Their recent 103-82 blowout win in Game 4 showcased their defensive prowess and offensive potential, shifting momentum in the tied series. With the Oklahoma City Thunder battling key injuries to Ajay Mitchell (out) and Jalen Williams (questionable), the Spurs are in a strong position to capitalize on the Thunder's diminished roster. Significant public money on the Spurs moneyline further suggests a perceived edge for the underdog.

picks.game: San Antonio Spurs vs Oklahoma City Thunder
picks.rationale: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander struggled considerably in Game 4, managing only 19 points on 6-of-15 shooting against San Antonio's adjusted defense. The Thunder's offensive challenges are exacerbated by the absence of Ajay Mitchell and the questionable status of Jalen Williams, forcing Gilgeous-Alexander to shoulder an even heavier burden against a focused defense. The Spurs' strategy of limiting double teams and contesting perimeter shots has proven effective in containing his scoring output. Shai Gilgeous-Alexander is active and playing for the Oklahoma City Thunder in this game.

The Vegas Golden Knights hold a commanding 3-0 series lead and have been in excellent form, winning 8 of their last 10 games overall. Colorado is facing significant injury concerns with Nathan MacKinnon playing through a lower-body injury and Valeri Nichushkin a game-time decision, while Vegas's Mark Stone has returned and contributed immediately. The Avalanche are also making a risky goalie change to Mackenzie Blackwood, who has a history of being pulled in playoff games. Furthermore, public and sharp money appear to be leaning towards Vegas, with the moneyline moving in their favor, indicating confidence in their ability to close out the series at home.

The series has seen relatively tight scoring, with game totals of 6, 4, and 8 goals, averaging 6 goals per game, with two games going under 6.5. The Golden Knights have demonstrated strong defensive play throughout the series, effectively limiting Colorado's high-powered offense. Vegas's starting goaltender, Carter Hart, has consistently outplayed the Avalanche's netminders, contributing to lower scoring. With Colorado's offensive production potentially hampered by injuries to key players and a new starting goalie in Mackenzie Blackwood, a tighter, lower-scoring contest is a strong possibility.

The Vegas Golden Knights have won all three games in this Western Conference Final series by at least a two-goal margin (4-2, 3-1, 5-3). Their recent form is exceptional, boasting 8 wins in their last 10 games, showcasing consistent performance. Colorado is struggling to find its footing in the series and is dealing with key injuries, including Nathan MacKinnon playing through a lower-body issue and Valeri Nichushkin being a game-time decision. Given Vegas's dominance in the series and their strong overall play, it is highly probable they will either win outright or lose by no more than one goal.

Despite trailing 0-3 in the series, the Colorado Avalanche are the Presidents' Trophy winners, boasting an elite roster with significant playoff experience. Facing elimination, the team is expected to play with unparalleled desperation and focus to extend their season, a common catalyst for strong performances in do-or-die situations. Star players Nathan MacKinnon and Cale Makar are confirmed to play, with MacKinnon expected to be used normally despite his recent injury. This combination of talent, high stakes, and a proven track record makes the Avalanche a strong contender for an outright victory in Game 4.

In a do-or-die Game 4, the Presidents' Trophy-winning Colorado Avalanche will unleash extreme desperation and their formidable offensive talent. With Nathan MacKinnon and Cale Makar leading the charge, a team of their caliber is capable of a multi-goal outburst when their season is on the line. Despite the series deficit, Colorado's strong regular season record and playoff experience indicate their potential for a dominant performance. The attractive odds of +205 offer significant value for a team with the offensive firepower and motivation to secure a decisive victory to stay alive in the series.

The Cincinnati Reds have a significant pitching advantage with Chase Burns (6-1, 1.83 ERA, 0.95 WHIP) on the mound against a New York Mets team that has not announced a starter and is struggling offensively. The Mets are 1-4 in their last 5 games and lost 7-2 to the Reds yesterday. The moneyline has seen sharp movement in Cincinnati's favor, climbing from -118 to -126, indicating professional money backing the Reds. Given the Mets' current offensive woes and key injuries to Francisco Lindor, Tylor Megill, and Justin Hagenman, the Reds are in a strong position to secure another victory.

picks.game: New York Yankees @ Kansas City Royals
picks.rationale: The New York Yankees are on a 12-game winning streak against the Kansas City Royals, including a 4-3 victory yesterday. New York boasts a significant pitching advantage with Cam Schlittler (6-2, 1.50 ERA) facing a Royals team expected to use a bullpen game after their starter went deep yesterday. The line movement reflects confidence in the Yankees, with their runline moving from -1.5 (-120) to -1.5 (-125), and public betting heavily favoring New York with 89.2% of tickets and 84.3% of the money. The Royals have struggled as underdogs this season, winning only 37.5% of such games.

The Milwaukee Brewers are in excellent recent form, boasting a 7-3 record in their last 10 games, and they defeated the St. Louis Cardinals 5-1 yesterday. Milwaukee's starting pitcher, Jacob Misiorowski, is pitching exceptionally well with a 5-2 record, 1.83 ERA, 0.83 WHIP, and 12 strikeouts in his last outing. The Brewers are at home and have demonstrated superior recent performance and a dominant starting pitcher, making them a confident pick against the Cardinals.

The Atlanta Braves have a strong head-to-head record against the Boston Red Sox in recent seasons, holding an 8-5 advantage in their last 13 matchups. The Braves are a significantly stronger team overall with a 36-18 season record compared to Boston's 22-30. The Red Sox are also 0-5 in their last 5 head-to-head games against the Braves, indicating recent dominance by Atlanta. Despite playing on the road, the Braves' superior form and historical advantage in recent contests make them a strong moneyline play.

Cincinnati Reds pitcher Chase Burns has been outstanding this season, boasting a 1.83 ERA and an elite 9.76 K/9 rate over 59.0 innings pitched. He is confirmed active and starting for the Reds today. Burns is facing a struggling New York Mets offense that has a .643 team OPS and is missing key players due to injury, including Francisco Lindor. Given Burns' demonstrated ability to miss bats at an elite clip and the Mets' offensive struggles, he is well-positioned to exceed 6.5 strikeouts in this matchup.
